candidates.why: Primary top-line revenue label; matches Total Revenue exactly in values and is the canonical revenue line.
Duplicate total of Revenue; kept as secondary priority in case labeling changes across years.
Represents a liability, not revenue; unsuitable as REVT and thus not selected in final_choice.

variables.notes: Revenue and Total Revenue are identical; Revenue chosen as primary with Total Revenue as fallback per priority rule.

candidates.why: Inventory asset, not an income statement cost-of-sales line; cannot be reliably used as COGS.
Another inventory/asset-type balance sheet item, not a P&L COGS line; not appropriate for COGS.

variables.notes: Income statement does not present a separate COGS line among candidates; available candidates are balance sheet inventory items, which should not be used as COGS. True COGS likely exists elsewhere in detailed statements, so manual review is warranted.

candidates.why: Large aggregate operating cost bucket that likely includes both direct costs and overhead; since gross profit is reported, this appears closer to total operating costs (including COGS) and is therefore excluded for pure SG&A mapping per instructions.
Classic SG&A-type overhead category; clearly part of selling/general/admin expenses and excludes COGS and D&A labels.
Other operating income/expense; while mixed, this is part of operating overhead rather than COGS and is an acceptable SG&A component under the guidelines.

variables.notes: Explicit SG&A label is absent; Administrative expenses and Net Other Operating Loss best represent operating overhead. Operating expenses likely includes COGS and is therefore excluded per rule to avoid mixing direct costs into SG&A.

candidates.why: Explicit R&D expense line; directly matches XRD definition.

variables.notes: Distinct R&D line present; no ambiguity.

candidates.why: Represents allocation of net income to minority shareholders, not interest expense.
Same as non-controlling interests on income statement; equity attribution, not interest.
Equity component, not an interest expense line.
Only candidate explicitly containing 'Interest'; likely represents interest portion of lease or similar financing costs and is the best available proxy for interest expense.
Interest income, opposite sign of interest expense; can serve as a secondary fallback if no expense is reported, but is not ideal.

variables.notes: No explicit income-statement interest expense line among candidates. Interest and Executory Costs is used as primary proxy; Interest Income retained as secondary priority fallback if needed by downstream logic.

candidates.why: Income from equity-method investments, not book equity balance.

candidates.row_label: Equity attributable to shareholders of the parent company
candidates.why: Represents equity belonging to parent shareholders only; valid book equity measure but excludes non-controlling interests.
Includes both equity attributable to shareholders of the parent and non-controlling interests; best single measure of total book equity (BE).
Technical balancing line, not an economic equity definition; not appropriate as BE when proper equity totals exist.
Represents total assets or total funding, not equity alone.

final_choice.row_label: Equity attributable to shareholders of the parent company

variables.notes: Total equity chosen as primary to capture full book equity including non-controlling interests; Equity attributable to shareholders of the parent company provided as secondary option.

candidates.why: Income statement allocation of profit to non-controlling shareholders; useful for flow measures but not the balance. Still included as fallback if balance sheet item is unavailable.
Alternative income statement label for non-controlling interests; appears duplicative of the preceding line, included as tertiary fallback.
Balance sheet minority interest (non-controlling interest) component of equity; best representation of MIB.

variables.notes: Balance sheet Non-controlling interests is primary for MIB as a stock measure; income statement lines are kept as fallbacks for robustness across time.
